drills.name: Par 18
instructions: Drop 18 balls in a ring around the green — different distances and lies.
Play each ball as its own hole: chip, then putt out.
Up and down (2 strokes) is par. Chip-in is 1. Three or more is over par.
Log strokes for every ball. Par for the round is 36 — lower is better.

drills.name: Clock Face
instructions: Imagine a clock around the hole. Chip from 12, 3, 6, and 9 o’clock.
Hit 3 balls from each station (12 total).
A make finishes inside a 6-foot circle around the hole.

drills.name: Circle Game
instructions: Draw or place a 6-foot circle around the hole.
Chip 12 balls from mixed lies outside the green.
Count every ball that finishes inside the circle.

drills.name: Gate Keeper
instructions: Build a gate just wider than the putter head, ~6 inches in front of the ball.
From 3 feet, stroke 10 putts through the gate and into the hole.
Both through the gate and in the hole counts as a make.

drills.name: Lag Zone
instructions: From about 20–30 feet, lag putts toward the hole.
Hit 10 putts. A make finishes inside a 3-foot circle.
Focus on pace — leave yourself a tap-in every time.

drills.name: 3-6-9 Pressure
instructions: Make 3 putts from 3 feet, then 3 from 6 feet, then 3 from 9 feet.
Log each putt as make or miss — 9 total.
Restart a distance only if you want extra pressure; score is still out of 9.

drills.name: Wedge Rings
instructions: Pick a target and set three rings (or use flags): close / mid / long.
Hit 10 wedges to the target.
Score each ball: 3 points inner, 2 middle, 1 outer, 0 miss. Max 30.

drills.name: Fairway Corridor
instructions: Pick two alignment sticks (or markers) as a fairway corridor toward a target.
Hit 10 drives or woods.
A make stays in the corridor the whole way — no start-line miss.

drills.name: Distance Ladder
instructions: Choose three carry distances (e.g. 40 / 60 / 80).
Hit 4 balls to each rung — 12 total.
A make lands inside a reasonable window for that distance (your call).
